global.title: WHO declares global health emergency over new Ebola outbreak - NBC News
global.summary: The WHO has now formally declared the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a global health emergency after more than 300 suspected cases and over 85 deaths.
global.whyItMatters: This is a major humanitarian and prayer concern, and it may shape pastoral intercession, missions awareness, and public health sensitivity in the congregation.

global.title: Millions on alert for severe weather with dangerous conditions expected - NBC News
global.summary: Millions across the U.S. are under alert for large hail, strong winds, possible tornadoes, and wildfire risk in parts of the upper Midwest.
global.whyItMatters: Severe weather stories help pastors frame prayers for protection, compassion, and practical care for displaced or anxious families.

global.title: San Francisco school using AI to reimagine learning - CBS News
global.summary: A San Francisco school is experimenting with AI as part of a new approach to teaching and learning, highlighting how quickly technology is reshaping education.
global.whyItMatters: This offers a fresh preaching and leadership angle on discernment, formation, and how churches guide families through rapid cultural change.

canadian.title: Multiple commencement speakers booed over AI comments - NBC News
canadian.summary: Several commencement speakers drew boos after making comments about AI, reflecting growing public unease about technology, work, and the future facing graduates.
canadian.whyItMatters: Canadian churches are discipling students and young adults in the same climate of uncertainty, making this a useful window into generational fears and hopes.

canadian.title: San Francisco school using AI to reimagine learning - CBS News
canadian.summary: A school’s use of AI in the classroom points to broader educational shifts that will likely influence Canadian parents, teachers, and students as well.
canadian.whyItMatters: Pastors in Canada are increasingly asked how faith communities should respond to AI in learning, formation, and child development.

canadian.title: Birding may hold key to keeping your brain sharp in old age, new study suggests - CBS News
canadian.summary: A new study suggests birding may help older adults keep their minds active and sharp, linking outdoor attention and engagement with healthy aging.
canadian.whyItMatters: This is a practical and encouraging angle for ministry with seniors, creation care, and whole-person discipleship.

local.title: ADUs: A solution to housing in your own backyard - CBS News
local.summary: A report on accessory dwelling units highlights one possible response to housing pressure by creating smaller homes on existing properties.
local.whyItMatters: Housing affordability remains a pressing issue in British Columbia, and this can inform pastoral care, community partnerships, and local advocacy.

local.title: Uncovering coded antisemitism online takes both human expertise and AI automation - Bozeman Daily Chronicle
local.summary: The piece argues that identifying online antisemitism requires both skilled human judgment and AI tools, especially as hateful language becomes more coded.
local.whyItMatters: Pastors need awareness of how antisemitism and online radicalization spread so they can teach discernment and protect community health.

persecutedChurch.title: Who on Earth Feels Threatened by a Nun? - Haaretz
persecutedChurch.summary: This new Haaretz opinion piece says attacks on Christians in Israel have become disturbingly common, describing a climate in which visible Christian presence can be treated with hostility.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: It gives a fresh angle on the vulnerability of Christian minorities in the Holy Land and can guide informed prayer and solidarity.

persecutedChurch.title: J'lem church leaders urge Knesset to address 'hate crimes' against Christians - Haaretz
persecutedChurch.summary: Jerusalem church leaders are urging the Knesset to respond to hate crimes against Christians, keeping attention on a pattern of pressure and intimidation.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: Though covered yesterday, it remains directly relevant to prayer for the persecuted church and awareness of mounting pressure on historic Christian communities.

leadership.title: Measuring presence instead of impact breaks leadership pipelines - HR Magazine
leadership.summary: This piece argues that healthy leadership cultures should reward outcomes, trust, and sustainability rather than mere visibility or long hours.
leadership.whyItMatters: It is a useful reminder for church leadership teams to build sustainable ministry expectations and avoid equating exhaustion with faithfulness.

goodNews.title: Community fundraises $57K to enable a Howard University senior to graduate on time - NBC News
goodNews.summary: A community rallied to raise $57,000 so a Howard University senior could clear financial barriers and graduate on time.
goodNews.whyItMatters: It is a hopeful picture of practical generosity and shared burden-bearing that pastors can celebrate and emulate.
